reviews.review_text: I was walking and saw a cat in a big tree. So I helped it and it jumped on me so that it could have a safe landing. But I think I startled it and it started to savagely attacking me until I was dead. 10/10 would help another big cat.

I sacrificed all my friends to tame a trex. I was so happy I finally got one. However, moments later he got ganked by an alpha raptor. I cried. I then recruited a team of 20, 20 feet long alligators to absolutely maul the alpha raptor. That revenge gave me more dopamine then beating my ♥♥♥♥ does. 10/10 game.

The game is playable. It runs 58-60fps on SteamDeck on mid-low setting, and runs 30-35fps on high-epic setting. The issue is even with the mid-low setting, the battery drainage is like an IPhone on life support. The loading time when I click to play single player take like 10-15 mins. It is pretty damm long.
As for story, I don't know, I'm new to this title. It's a survival game, I honestly don't expect much from the story.
As for theme, adventure always tag along with beauty, horror, and surprise elements. This game got all of that.
As for game mechanics, it is confusing and too complex sometimes. I feel like if I quit the game for like 2 or 3 years, the moment I come back I'll be a caveman.
Overall, the game is fine. Graphic is ok,  gameplay is fine, quite confusing at some point thou. The issue with this game is loading time is insanely long.

Went back to this with a couple of friends to complete the story. Got through Beta King Titan on Extinction then my character got deleted after beating the boss and ascending. Don't waste your time with this game. It will inevitably crash on downloading your character back to the map after defeating a boss and will delete said character. 

This Happened to all 3 of us in the group across: The Island for one friend, SE for another, and finally Extinction for myself. 

Avoid this game. Every expansion gets worse than the previous.
C'est un très bon jeu mais mon ancienne config ne me permettait pas d'y joué convenable donc je l'ai fait rembourser mais je pense bien le racheter
i use to love this game but its is now completely dead, no official servers, only servers are unofficial and they randomly close after spending time and effort on it
A lot of beautiful assets come together for some really cool world building. But frankly the game feels bloated in UI, mechanics, and general gameplay.

great concept, terrible devs. Game had a lot of potential but devs have no idea how to optimize the game until they eventually just abandon this game and head on with the next version that is also just almost as bad as evolved.

This game is a pittiful pit of despair and agony, onlly comparable to the last circles of hell. A buggy, addictive mess that keeps pulling me back in everytime I'm about to forget it. I had it on epic games, uninstalled it there but somehow it crossed my mind to purchase it on steam, I cannot escape this game, no matter how many times I uninstall it, change my gaming console to a PC and back or switch to another gaming platform. Steer away from this game, cause even if it deletes your whole game save TWICE over a crash or can't ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ keep your items stored in boxes without making them disappear, You will still find yourself falling into this torture machine disguised as a game, even if you had 600h hours of pure bad time on epic games and came back to play it on steam, you will not learn from the mistake of downloading it, you cannot quit it whenever you want, as much as you want to believe it. Playing this game is comparable to smoking meth, just without any of the good parts and with all the cons: You lose your grip on reality, forget family and friends, your mental and physical health deteriorate as you find yourself needing more and more every day, even if the experience of consuming this madness will ruin your social and mental life in every possible way, without providing any joy that you're searching for in a video game, which this torture chamber of agony pretends to be. I'll proceed to spend hundreds of hours on this game, I'm already way in too deep, but, even as an atheist, I do hope that this review will help you find god, because only he can save you from going down the darkest path your life could take you through, by playing something that You would rather rip every single nerve out of your skin instead.

this game was very fun in single player. i spent lots of time trying to get as much stuff as possible, but later decided to cheat which led to the game getting boring. ark is fun but multiplayer (although the most played aspect) is completely the opposite, basically torture. do recommend if anyone somehow hasnt played this game yet, but make sure you have a whole data center to be able to download it.

I had a great time as long as we stuck to PvE. But this game is more like a simulator where you buy 12 dogs, a parrot, a hedgehog, and a worm. You have to play it full-time and basically do nothing else.

On the plus side, you can meet a lot of nice autistic people because there are dinosaurs there.

Buggy, laggy game, there's not even a hint of life. NPCs of various kinds run back and forth with no apparent purpose. The creatures don't behave naturally. They attack nearby creatures only when you get very close. During my nearly three days of playing, I got stuck in way too many places, which meant I had to start the game over and over again

Even in 2026, ARK: Survival Evolved is still one of the craziest survival games ever made. With all DLCs installed, the game feels massive — every map has its own atmosphere, creatures, challenges, and story. From surviving on The Island to exploring Aberration, Extinction, Genesis, and Fjordur, the amount of content is honestly insane.

The dinosaur taming system is still unmatched. Building giant bases, breeding powerful creatures, exploring caves, fighting bosses, and surviving with friends creates moments no other game really delivers. The graphics may show their age sometimes, and the game can still be buggy, but ARK’s scale and freedom make up for it.

What makes ARK special is that every playthrough becomes its own story. One day you’re getting destroyed by a raptor, the next day you’re flying across the map on a mutated wyvern army. Few games give that feeling of progression.

With all extras and DLCs, this is easily hundreds or even thousands of hours of gameplay. If you enjoy survival, dinosaurs, open-world exploration, and co-op chaos, ARK is still worth playing in 2026.

Form me, it worth all cents i have paid for this game in all DLC.

i wasn't sure to give this game a recommendation or not but i decided do go with it, the game itself its buggy and has a lot to desire but the price went down a lot since the new game released so its fine, the gameplay idea is where it shine, hand down best survival ever, the taming method is not that inclusive but its time and resource consuming and for me its a good thing, it mean u have to grind to get good dino and progress, and at the end of its life cicle they started to add different taming metods for some of the dinos that keep the game fresh and unique and with a lot of different maps most of which free with a large number of bosses is the recipe for an almost never ending and fullfilling experience, and to top it all the unofficial servers give u a chance to play online how u like it cause u can choose a server with the setting u want:
For the gameplay itself i cannot be that lenient, its buggy to say the least, dino and players clipping walls makes every aspect of the game harder than it already is, caves like lava golem on rag with half of the spiders in the walls, a number of never ending way to clip outside map and possibly dying where u can lose everything or getting urself raided from the wall where u didnt place any defences make this game annoying to play as to be expected, 4 week of hard work could be wiped in 1 hour.
For the state of the game at the moment i cannot say anything good either the game has been taken over by snail that reales a DLC that broke the game and made it unplayable and even if u had the deluxe edition u cannot get it for free that was not the case with every one of the other 5 paid DLC map, luckly u can play in the PRE-ACQUATICA version that just like the name hints its pre update.
In the end this game is at the end of its life, they stopped the Official server leaving u only Unofficial and Offline option, it makes u choose to either play alone, pay for a server or pray that the server u are going to join do not stop existing in the middle of ur playtrough.
its not a game i usually suggest but if u can see beyond the negative of this game the idea is like i said earlier hand down the best there is, sadly it got ruined by bad decision and with the neverending number of bug and exploit.
for the new gen game, ascended, its almost the same with just some new map some bad rebalancing and even more paywalls, its not worth to get over this unless u want to play online

There's probably not much new I can say about this game since most people are already familiar with it. All I can say is that it's definitely worth your attention. The game is well-crafted, packed with content, and offers plenty of hours of entertainment.

That said, it does have its flaws. The game can occasionally crash, optimization isn't always the best, and you may need to spend some time tweaking the settings to get smooth performance without sacrificing too much visual quality.

Despite these issues, it's a great game for the price and offers excellent value considering the amount of content available.
This game is very addicting in an INCRIDIBLY good way! Only play if you have the time to!

First they gave the game out for free. I missed the window, was pretty bummed but still bought it again, because it's a good game and a free next-gen upgrade with a new engine was promised. Well that never happened and now the 'new' remastered version costs $39.99.

After charging people money for something they promised to do for free, in July 2025 they released a new DLC map and update for this game (the discontinued old legacy version of ARK) that functionally breaks the game. As in the game does not even load anymore just sits at infinite loading screens. So bad that Steam had to step in and offer a 'pre-aquatica' update branch of the game, just so people can actually play the game they paid money for. - After they set fire to the game, they just abandoned it. 1 year later and the most current newest update available for this game on Steam fundamentally does not work with my hardware. (which is all 2020+ hardware btw) I still have to use the pre-aquatica update branch to even get the game to function properly.

I don't even know where to begin on how broken this game is. My friends wanted to me get the game for YEARS. So I finally caved in since it was on sale for $10. A 57 gig install took 6 hours because of the way the game is designed to install a bit, patch those files, install a bit more, stop and patch those files and so on. Just to finally find out the game will crash on the main menu non stop and wreck my PC because it's files are , "Broken" Because I only bought the base game and not all the DLC's that make the GAME PLAYABLE!! SO i had to revert to a previous version of the game which is ANOTHER 15 gig update of install a few files and patch them. I'm about to request a refund for this excuse of a game. I am a calm and laid back person but this game has been frustrating to levels I didn't know existed.

This game however, is one of a kind. i dont like the pvp aspect but i do really enjoy that i can explore and loot and build a dino army to take on bosses. the taming, food requirements, crafting, and blueprint progression are awesome and tickle my brain very nicely. there are bugs that are kinda annoying but nothing that makes it an unplayable or unenjoyable experience. also the mod support is great and if you can think of it, it probably exists already.
